skill.name: email-assistant
skill.description: Guides assistants through reading, drafting, replying to, and sending Gmail messages with explicit user approval.
skill.summary: Draft and manage concise Gmail responses with approval checks.
skill.author: ClementWalter

security_audit.summary: Static external command and weak cryptography alerts were false positives caused by markdown code fences and ordinary prose in SKILL.md. The skill has no executable code, network endpoint, or shell command, but it documents 1Password item names for Gmail account selection.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged triple backtick fences and inline tool names as Ruby or shell execution. These lines are documentation examples and Gmail MCP tool names, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside markdown text blocks or inline code formatting. No script file, shell invocation, or command interpolation exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: 1Password Item Names Are Documented
low_findings.description: The skill tells users to use named 1Password items for the account parameter. This exposes account item labels but does not reveal passwords, tokens, or vault contents.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly contains two 1Password item names. The concern is limited because only labels are visible and no credential values are present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged ordinary description and example text as weak cryptography. No hashing, encryption, checksum, or cryptographic API usage appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are frontmatter prose and a French email example. They contain no cryptographic operation or algorithm selection.

content.value_statement: Email tasks can lose context, tone, and approval discipline. This skill provides a clear Gmail workflow for concise drafts, replies, and sends.
actual_capabilities: Lists recent emails before selecting a message to read.
Reads full email content when the user chooses a message.
Drafts concise replies in the original email language.
Formats drafts with recipient, subject, attachment, and body sections.
Requires explicit user approval before replying or sending.
Supports choosing between documented work and personal Gmail accounts.
limitations: Requires compatible Gmail MCP tools to list, read, search, reply, and send.
Does not send emails without explicit confirmation from the user.
Does not define attachment discovery or file validation behavior.
Includes fixed account item names that may need local adjustment.

use_cases.title: Quick Inbox Review
use_cases.description: Review recent Gmail messages, open selected emails, and summarize long threads before taking action.

use_cases.title: Concise Customer Replies
use_cases.description: Draft short responses that match the customer's language and preserve the thread context.

use_cases.title: Approved Work Email Sending
use_cases.description: Prepare a new message with recipient, subject, body, and attachment details before user confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Check Recent Email
prompt_templates.prompt: List my recent emails, show the sender and subject, and ask me which one to open.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Reply
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the selected email and draft a short reply in the same language. Show it to me before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Search and Respond
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my emails for the latest message about the invoice, summarize it, and prepare a concise response.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Account-Specific Send
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my work Gmail account to draft a new email with the recipient, subject, and attachment details I provide.

output_examples.input: Reply to the selected French email with the requested document attached.
output_examples.output: A short French draft with recipient, subject, attachment name, body text, and a confirmation question before sending.

output_examples.input: Show my recent emails and help me answer the most urgent one.
output: A recent email list with senders and subjects.
A summary of the chosen email.
A concise draft reply shown for approval.

output_examples.input: Send a new message to a client from my personal Gmail account.
output_examples.output: A prepared message with recipient, subject, body, account choice, and a request for explicit send approval.

best_practices: Review every draft before approving a send or reply action.
Confirm the Gmail account when work and personal contexts could overlap.
Provide the desired tone, attachment names, and recipient details upfront.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to send email without reviewing the draft.
Do not use it when the Gmail MCP tools are unavailable.
Do not rely on it to verify attachment contents unless another tool checks them.

faq.question: Does this skill send emails automatically?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the assistant to show the draft and wait for explicit user approval before sending.

faq.question: Can it reply in French?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill tells the assistant to match the language of the original email.

faq.question: Which Gmail actions does it describe?
faq.answer: It describes listing emails, reading emails, searching emails, replying in a thread, and sending new emails.

faq.question: Does it require Gmail MCP tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow depends on Gmail MCP tools such as list_emails, read_email, reply_email, and send_email.

faq.question: Can it handle attachments?
faq.answer: It includes attachment fields in drafts and mentions replies or sends with attachments, but it does not validate files.

faq.question: Is any credential data included?
faq.answer: No credential values are included. The skill does list two 1Password item names for account selection.
